properties.name: Kathmandu
properties.description: Capital of Nepal and cultural heart of the country. Home to seven UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

properties.name: Pokhara
properties.description: Nepal's tourism capital. Gateway to the Annapurna region, known for Phewa Lake and Himalayan panoramas.

properties.name: Lumbini
properties.description: Birthplace of Gautama Buddha (c. 563 BCE). UNESCO World Heritage Site and sacred Buddhist pilgrimage destination.

properties.name: Mt. Everest
properties.description: World's highest mountain at 8,848.86 m above sea level. Called Sagarmatha in Nepali.

properties.name: Annapurna I
properties.description: World's 10th highest mountain at 8,091 m. The first 8,000m peak ever climbed (1950).

properties.name: Namche Bazaar
properties.description: Gateway to the Everest region. The main Sherpa trading hub at 3,440 m altitude.

properties.name: Everest Base Camp
properties.description: South Base Camp of Mount Everest at 5,364 m. Starting point for Everest summit expeditions.

properties.name: Annapurna Base Camp
properties.description: High-altitude sanctuary surrounded by the Annapurna massif at 4,130 m. Iconic 360° mountain views.

properties.name: Biratnagar
properties.description: Second largest city of Nepal, capital of Koshi Province and the country's main industrial hub.

properties.name: Janakpur
properties.description: Capital of Madhesh Province, mythological birthplace of Sita Devi and site of the Janaki Temple.

properties.name: Rara Lake
properties.description: Nepal's largest lake at 10.8 km². Located in Rara National Park at 2,990 m. A pristine alpine gem.

properties.name: Pashupatinath Temple
properties.description: Nepal's most sacred Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva. UNESCO World Heritage Site on the banks of the Bagmati River.

properties.name: Bhaktapur Durbar Square
properties.description: Well-preserved medieval city and UNESCO World Heritage Site with remarkable Newari architecture.

properties.name: Dhangadhi
properties.description: Capital of Sudurpashchim Province in far-western Nepal.

properties.name: Phewa Lake
properties.description: Nepal's second-largest lake in Pokhara Valley. Offers stunning reflections of the Annapurna range.
